rationale: Title Y flows more naturally by placing the patient population qualifier at the end as a clause rather than embedding 'Universal Hemolysis' mid-title, improving clarity and matching Heart Rhythm's typical concise comparative-study structure.
loser_weakness: Title X's insertion of 'Universal' before 'Hemolysis' is an unusual modifier that reads awkwardly and interrupts the parallel structure, reducing clarity and appeal without adding informative value.
